gap_patterns.message: A code editor and a vector database aren't directly connected. You need an orchestration layer to build the retrieval pipeline, and a model provider to generate embeddings and responses.
Your editor doesn't call model APIs directly — you need an SDK or orchestration layer to structure how your application makes LLM calls.
Observability traces LLM calls — without a model provider in the stack there's nothing to observe.
A design tool and a vector database sit at opposite ends of the stack. You need an editor to build the app, an orchestration layer to wire retrieval, and a model provider for embeddings.
A design tool generates UI components — to make them call an LLM you need an editor to build the app and an SDK to structure the API calls.
Observability traces LLM calls, but a design tool only generates UI — the full app stack needs to exist between them.
Agents need a model to generate embeddings before they can store or retrieve from a vector database.
A spec defines what to build — to call a model from it you need a code editor to implement the app and an SDK to structure the LLM calls.
A spec tool and a vector database have no direct connection — you need the full app stack between them to make retrieval work.
Code review tooling and a vector database operate in completely different parts of the stack — the connection runs through the application that uses the vector DB.
Eval tools measure LLM output quality — without a model provider in the stack there are no outputs to evaluate.
MCP servers give agents tool access, but connecting them to a vector database requires an orchestration layer to build the retrieval pipeline and a model to generate embeddings.
MCP servers extend agent capabilities, but observability traces LLM calls — you need a model provider to have anything to trace.
Eval tools measure AI output quality — a design tool only generates UI components. You need the full AI stack between them before there's anything to evaluate.

traversal_config.note: For tool pairs with no direct relationship, perform BFS through the relationships graph following only integrates-with and commonly-paired edges. Surface intermediate nodes as missing bridge tools, grouped by their slot. If no path is found within max_depth, fall back to gap_patterns lookup by slot pair.
